notes: Multiple cacay oil brands already on Amazon (Cacay Naturals, CACAYE, Arles Biocosmetics, etc). Well-established import presence, no brand gap.
Coffee sugar scrub category saturated - OGX, Tree Hut, BUM, Lalicious all have established coffee+sugar scrub products. Panela-specific branding not differentiated enough.
Aloe vera gel market dominated by big brands. Stick applicator format interesting but not compelling enough vs tubes/bottles.
Eye patch market crowded with K-beauty brands. Chamomile-specific positioning not unique - many eye patches already use chamomile extract.

product_name: Overnight Vaseline face mask slugging balm
notes: Slugging trend already commercialized by multiple brands (Aquaphor, CeraVe Healing Ointment, La Roche-Posay Cicaplast). Stick format not compelling enough.
Onion for hair growth is niche folk remedy. US consumers unlikely to adopt onion-scented hair products even if odorless. Cultural barrier.

product_name: Avocado papaya freeze-dried hair mask powder
notes: Hair mask market crowded. Freeze-dried format interesting but avocado/papaya masks already common in DIY beauty. Not compelling enough as standalone brand.

product_name: Egg white sugar face mask powder
notes: Powdered egg white for face masks is niche. US consumers prefer ready-to-use masks. DIY powder format doesn't solve a real pain point.
Coffee soap market saturated. Many artisan soap brands already do coffee exfoliating bars. Colombian origin story not differentiated enough.
Oatmeal soap extremely common. No differentiation from existing brands (Aveeno, artisan soap makers, etc).
Calendula soap common in natural beauty space. No compelling twist vs existing brands.

product_name: Colombian coffee body scrub with guarana
notes: Coffee body scrub market very crowded. Pre-workout angle interesting but not compelling enough vs established brands.
Replicating saliva enzymes for lash growth is scientifically complex, likely requires clinical trials. Too much R&D for first product.
Body oil market crowded. Tropical fruit scents not unique - many brands already do guava, passionfruit, etc oils.
Subscription box model requires high customer acquisition cost, inventory risk, and operational complexity. Not a low-capital start.

product_name: Massage candle with Colombian coffee scent
notes: Massage candles common (Ember Wellness, etc). Coffee scent not differentiated enough to build a brand around.
Building a multi-SKU serum line like The Ordinary requires significant formulation expertise, clinical testing, and capital. Too complex for first product.

product_name: Morning saliva enzyme acne spot treatment
notes: Same as lash serum - replicating saliva enzymes is scientifically complex. Requires R&D and clinical validation.
